params.text:
Keep the closed system closed

The drainage system works best when it stays sealed. Every unnecessary break in the system is a chance for germs to enter, so the golden rule is to keep the connection between the catheter and the drainage bag closed unless there is a clinical reason to open it. Urine is emptied through the tap at the bottom of the bag, never by disconnecting the bag from the catheter.

  • Do not disconnect: keep the catheter and bag connected.
  • Empty via the tap: use the outlet tap, not the connection.
  • Only open: when clinically necessary and delegated to you.

Bags and how they connect

Many people wear a leg bag during the day, held on the thigh or calf, which is emptied through its tap. At night, a larger night bag is usually attached to the outlet of the leg bag so the person does not have to wake to empty it, and this keeps the day system closed. Some people use a catheter valve instead of a bag, opening it regularly to drain urine.

Whatever the arrangement, follow the person's care plan and change bags only as directed and only if this has been delegated to you. If a bag is leaking, disconnected, or the wrong type appears to be in use, do not improvise — report it to the nurse. Keeping the right, closed arrangement in place protects the person from infection.

params.taskDescription: Drag each word into the correct gap.
params.textField: Urine should be emptied through the *tap* at the bottom of the bag, never by *disconnecting* the bag. At night a larger *night* bag is attached to keep the system *closed*.
